# Driftpin

Driftpin helps you 

```bash
$ ls -a # empty folder
. ..
$ drift status
You are currently not in a Driftpin-tracked project. To start tracking this folder, run `drift init`
$ drift init
$ ls -a
.  ..  drift.pin  main.pin.xml
$ cat main.pin.xml
<spec name="my-project" version="0.2.0">
</spec>
$ cat drift.pin
# drift.pin — auto-generated by Driftpin. Do not edit.

[site]

[state]
$ drift status
Clauses: 0
Markers: 0
Issues: 0
$ drift add
Added new marker with id a1a1a1a1. You can start using it immediately.
For example, 
<!-- #F id:a1a1a1 clause.id -->
// #F id:a1a1a1 clause.id
# #F id:a1a1a1 clause.id
$ cat drift.pin
# drift.pin — auto-generated by Driftpin. Do not edit.

[markers]
a1a1a1a1

[site]

[state]
$ drift status
Clauses: 0
Markers: 1
Issues: 1
Issues that need your attention:
    unused marker: a1a1a1a1
# User adds a new clause "foo"
$ cat main.pin.xml
<spec name="my-project" version="0.2.0">
  <clause id="foo">The widget must fizz the buzz.</clause>
</spec>
$ drift status
Clauses: 1
Markers: 1
Issues: 2
Issues that need your attention:
    unused marker: a1a1a1a1 (connected to 0 clauses)
    untracked clause: foo
$ drift connect a1a1a1a1 foo
Error: `foo` is not tracked. Use the `drift term track foo` instead.
$ cat drift.pin
# drift.pin — auto-generated by Driftpin. Do not edit.

[markers]
a1a1a1a1 foo

[site]

[state]
$ drift status
 Clauses: 1
Markers: 1
Issues: 2
Issues that need your attention:
    unused marker: a1a1a1a1 (connected to 1 clause)
    unused clause: foo (connected to 1 marker)
$ echo 'package main

// #F id:a1a1a1a1 foo
func fizz(b string) string {
    return "fizz:" + b
}' > widget.go
$ drift status
Clauses: 1
Markers: 1
Issues: 1
Issues that need your attention:
    drifted marker: a1a1a1a1 (connected to 1 clause)
$ drift add
Added new marker with id b2b2b2b2. You can start using it immediately.
For example, 
<!-- #F id:b2b2b2b2 clause.id -->
// #F id:b2b2b2b2 clause.id
# #F id:b2b2b2b2 clause.id
$ drift status
Clauses: 1
Markers: 2
Issues: 1
Issues that need your attention:
    drifted marker: a1a1a1a1 (connected to 1 clause)
    unused marker: b2b2b2b2 (connected to 0 clauses)
$ echo 'package main

// #F id:b2b2b2b2 foo
func buzz(s string) string {
    return "buzz:" + s
}' > buzzer.go
$ drift status
Clauses: 1
Markers: 2
Issues: 2
Issues that need your attention:
    drifted marker: a1a1a1a1 (connected to 1 clauses)
    disconnected marker: b1b1b1b1 (connected to 0 clauses)
$ drift status
Clauses: 1
Markers: 2
Issues: 2
Issues that need your attention:
    drifted marker: a1a1a1a1 (connected to 1 clauses)
    disconnected marker: b1b1b1b1 (connected to 0 clauses)
# user edits the foo clause in main.pin.xml
$ cat main.pin.xml
<spec name="my-project" version="0.2.0">
  <clause id="foo">The widget must fizz the buzz and return the result.</clause>
</spec>
$ drift status
Clauses: 1
Markers: 3
Issues: 1
Issues that need your attention:
    drifted spec: foo
$ drift ok a1a1a1a1
Clauses: 1
Markers: 3
Issues: 0
# user creates yet another file and reuses marker id a1a1a1a1
$ drift status
Clauses: 1
Markers: 3
Issues: 1
Issues that need your attention:
    duplicate marker: a1a1a1a1 appears in /path/to/file.txt:3 and /path/to/other.txt:5
    this is a critical error. each marker id must be unique across all files.
    to resolve: remove the duplicate marker from one file, or run 'drift add' to generate a new marker id
$ drift add
Added new marker with id d4d4d4d4. You can start using it immediately.
# user replaces the duplicate marker in other.txt with d4d4d4d4
$ drift status
Clauses: 1
Markers: 4
Issues: 0
```
